# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

require 'shellwords'
require 'tempfile'
require 'open3'

class ImageConverterController < ApplicationController
  # Renders the image upload form
  def new
    # No instance variables needed for a simple form
  end

  # Handles the image upload and conversion
  def create
    uploaded_file = params[:image]
    desired_format = params[:format]
    desired_size = params[:size]

    # --- Input Validation and Security ---
    unless uploaded_file && uploaded_file.respond_to?(:original_filename)
      return render plain: "No image file uploaded.", status: :bad_request
    end

    # Whitelist allowed output formats to prevent command injection or unexpected file types
    allowed_formats = %w[png jpg jpeg gif webp tiff bmp]
    unless allowed_formats.include?(desired_format.to_s.downcase)
      return render plain: "Unsupported format '#{desired_format}'. Allowed formats: #{allowed_formats.join(', ')}", status: :bad_request
    end

    # Validate size string: allow formats like "100x100", "50%", "100" (width only)
    # This regex ensures only numeric dimensions or percentages are passed.
    # ImageMagick's geometry string is complex; this is a common subset.
    unless desired_size.to_s.match?(/\A(\d+x\d+|\d+%|\d+)\z/)
      return render plain: "Invalid size format '#{desired_size}'. Expected formats like '100x100', '50%', or '100'.", status: :bad_request
    end
    # --- End Input Validation ---

    input_file_path = nil
    output_file_path = nil

    begin
      # Create a temporary file for the uploaded image
      input_temp_file = Tempfile.new(['uploaded_image', File.extname(uploaded_file.original_filename)])
      input_temp_file.binmode # Ensure binary mode for image data
      input_temp_file.write(uploaded_file.read)
      input_temp_file.close # Close the file handle so ImageMagick can access it
      input_file_path = input_temp_file.path

      # Create a temporary file for the converted image
      output_temp_file = Tempfile.new(['converted_image', ".#{desired_format}"])
      output_temp_file.close # Close immediately; ImageMagick will write directly to this path
      output_file_path = output_temp_file.path

      # --- Construct and execute ImageMagick 'convert' command ---
      # IMPORTANT: Shellwords.shellescape is CRUCIAL to prevent command injection.
      # It safely escapes strings for use in a shell command.
      escaped_input_path = Shellwords.shellescape(input_file_path)
      escaped_output_path = Shellwords.shellescape(output_file_path)
      escaped_size = Shellwords.shellescape(desired_size) # Even validated inputs should be escaped if they might contain special shell characters.

      # The 'convert' command: input_path -> resize -> output_path
      command = "convert #{escaped_input_path} -resize #{escaped_size} #{escaped_output_path}"

      Rails.logger.info "Executing ImageMagick command: #{command}"

      # Execute the command using Open3.capture2e for robustness
      # Open3.capture2e returns stdout_and_stderr_output, status_object
      output_stream, status = Open3.capture2e(command)

      unless status.success?
        Rails.logger.error "ImageMagick conversion failed for command: #{command}"
        Rails.logger.error "ImageMagick output: #{output_stream}"
        return render plain: "Image conversion failed. Details: #{output_stream}", status: :internal_server_error
      end

      # --- Send the converted image as a response ---
      # `send_file` handles reading the file and setting appropriate HTTP headers.
      send_file output_file_path,
                filename: "converted_image.#{desired_format}",
                type: "image/#{desired_format == 'jpg' ? 'jpeg' : desired_format}",
                disposition: 'inline' # 'inline' to display in browser, 'attachment' to force download

    rescue => e
      # Catch any other unexpected errors during file operations or processing
      Rails.logger.error "Error during image conversion: #{e.message}"
      Rails.logger.error e.backtrace.join("\n") # Log the full backtrace for debugging
      render plain: "An error occurred during image processing: #{e.message}", status: :internal_server_error
    ensure
      # --- Clean up temporary files ---
      # Ensure temporary files are deleted, even if an error occurred.
      File.delete(input_file_path) if input_file_path && File.exist?(input_file_path)
      File.delete(output_file_path) if output_file_path && File.exist?(output_file_path)
    end
  end
end

# config/routes.rb
# Rails.application.routes.draw do
#   get 'image_converter/new', to: 'image_converter#new', as: 'new_image_converter'
#   post 'image_converter', to: 'image_converter#create', as: 'image_converter'
# end

# app/views/image_converter/new.html.erb
# <!DOCTYPE html>
# <html>
# <head>
#   <title>Image Converter</title>
#   <%= csrf_meta_tags %>
#   <%= csp_meta_tag %>
# </head>
# <body>
#   <h1>Upload and Convert Image</h1>
#
#   <%= form_with url: image_converter_path, multipart: true, local: true do |form| %>
#     <div>
#       <%= form.label :image, "Select Image:" %><br>
#       <%= form.file_field :image %>
#     </div>
#     <br>
#     <div>
#       <%= form.label :format, "Output Format:" %><br>
#       <%= form.select :format, options_for_select(['png', 'jpg', 'gif', 'webp', 'tiff', 'bmp'], 'png') %>
#     </div>
#     <br>
#     <div>
#       <%= form.label :size, "Output Size (e.g., 200x200, 50%, 300):" %><br>
#       <%= form.text_field :size, value: '200x200' %>
#     </div>
#     <br>
#     <div>
#       <%= form.submit "Convert Image" %>
#     </div>
#   <% end %>
# </body>
# </html>
